canonical_invariant_definitions.name: Structured concurrency: every task is owned by exactly one region
canonical_invariant_definitions.statement: Task ownership is single-region and structurally preserved across spawn/schedule/cancel/close transitions.

canonical_invariant_definitions.name: Region close = quiescence
canonical_invariant_definitions.statement: A close-complete state implies no live children/subregions and no pending finalizers or obligations.

canonical_invariant_definitions.name: Cancellation is a protocol: request -> drain -> finalize (idempotent)
canonical_invariant_definitions.statement: Cancellation progresses through modeled protocol states with bounded cleanup and idempotent request semantics.

canonical_invariant_definitions.name: Losers are drained after races
canonical_invariant_definitions.statement: Non-winning race branches must be cancelled and fully drained before completion is considered safe.

canonical_invariant_definitions.name: No obligation leaks
canonical_invariant_definitions.statement: Obligations are explicitly resolved (committed/aborted/leaked), and unresolved obligations block close.

canonical_invariant_definitions.name: No ambient authority
canonical_invariant_definitions.statement: Effects require explicit capabilities; unauthorized ambient effects are disallowed.

invariants.name: Structured concurrency: every task is owned by exactly one region
invariants.statement: Task membership and region relationships remain structurally consistent through spawn/schedule/cancel/close transitions.

invariants.name: Region close = quiescence
invariants.statement: If a close step completes, all child tasks/subregions/finalizers are drained and obligations are not pending.

invariants.name: Cancellation is a protocol: request -> drain -> finalize (idempotent)
invariants.statement: Cancellation transitions progress through the modeled states and terminate within bounded potential.

invariants.name: Losers are drained after races
invariants.statement: In a race, non-winning branches must be cancelled and fully drained before completion is considered safe.
gaps: No direct Lean theorem currently states and proves race-loser full-drain semantics.

invariants.name: No obligation leaks
invariants.statement: Obligations are either committed/aborted/leaked with explicit state transitions and ledger updates; unresolved obligations block close.
gaps: A single global theorem stating zero leaked obligations across all reachable executions is not yet present.

invariants.name: No ambient authority
invariants.statement: Effects flow through explicit capabilities; unauthorized actions are rejected by type/capability constraints.
gaps: Capability discipline is heavily tested in Rust, but no dedicated Lean proof family exists yet for ambient-authority exclusion.
